author: jochembrouwer
content:
Hiya! We are targeting to move to the state-actor tool which will also help so you can run these benchs locally more easily (and are less dependent on us 🙂 ). I intend to start running these (updated) suites again. If you want you can bring it up later at the benchmark call today.

If you want more info on the details of these tests and how they differ let me know, happy to explain 🙂

author: matkt0
content:
nice thanks if we can run them again it will be interesting 

we can run the tests locally but we still need the test suites updated and the snapshot

author: skylenet
content:
Yes, that's where state-actor comes in play. It will be able to generate a snapshot on demand. 
And then we'll be able to also generate the payloads on top of the generated snapshot using the `fill-stateful`  from the execution-specs repo. 

This functionality will all be wrapped into a simple `build` command on the benchmarkoor side. There's gonna be a config file defining the snapshot and which payloads should be build. It's gonna be a single command that builds the state(snapshot) + builds the payloads. 

This will make it way easier to reproduce and run the full benchmark environment end-to-end ( Snapshot generation -> Benchmark payload generation -> Benchmark Execution ).
